message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #3 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #3 (HTMX focus management after delete) committed, pushed, CI GREEN (d86e6b7, 61 tests).

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Changes:
- New static/js/todo-focus.js — captures next/prev sibling before HTMX delete swap, moves focus after settle
- templates/todos.html — includes the script
- tests/api/todos.rs — new test for script serving
- Focus targets: next sibling > previous sibling > add-todo input (if list empty)
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the changes in commit d86e6b7.

input.description: Show files changed in Task #3 commit

content.content:

content.text: Let me also verify the base template has a `scripts` block and check how `todo-item` class is used.

content.content:
templates/todos.html:11:{% block scripts %} templates/base.html:33: {% block scripts %}{% endblock %} templates/register.html:5:{% block scripts %} templates/todo_item.html:1:
  • templates/todo_item.html:2:
    {{ todo.title }} templates/todo_item.html:10: templates/todo_item.html:15: ✕ templates/todo_item.html:19:

toolUseResult.content:
templates/todos.html:11:{% block scripts %} templates/base.html:33: {% block scripts %}{% endblock %} templates/register.html:5:{% block scripts %} templates/todo_item.html:1:
  • templates/todo_item.html:2:
    {{ todo.title }} templates/todo_item.html:10: templates/todo_item.html:15: ✕ templates/todo_item.html:19:

content.text: Good. The `todo-item` class is on the `<li>` elements in `todo_item.html`, which is exactly what the focus management script targets. Now let me send my consent.

input.content:
**I consent to Task #3 (d86e6b7).**

From a domain architecture perspective, this is a pure progressive enhancement -- no domain, service, or infrastructure changes. My review notes:

1. **No domain layer impact.** The delete handler (`post_delete_todo` in routes/todos.rs) is unchanged. The server still returns an empty body for HTMX delete swaps. The focus management is purely client-side behavior responding to the existing HTMX swap lifecycle.

2. **The detection logic is sound.** The script checks two conditions to identify a delete operation: (a) the target has class `todo-item` (matching `todo_item.html:1`), and (b) the response body is empty (which is what `post_delete_todo` returns for HTMX requests). This correctly distinguishes deletes from toggles or other swaps that return HTML fragments.

3. **Focus target priority is logically correct.** Next sibling > previous sibling > add-todo input. This follows natural reading order and handles the "list is now empty" edge case by falling back to the `#title` input, which is the right call -- the user's next action after deleting the last item would be to add a new one.

4. **The `pendingFocusTarget` pattern is a clean way to bridge the before-swap and after-settle lifecycle.** Capture the target while DOM siblings are still available, apply focus after the swap has settled. The variable is nulled after use to prevent stale references.

5. **`tabindex="-1"` for programmatic focus on non-interactive `<li>` elements** is correct -- the todo items are `<li>` elements which aren't natively focusable.

No domain concerns. Clean progressive enhancement.
input.summary: Consent to Task #3 - pure progressive enhancement

This correctly distinguishes deletes from toggles or other swaps that return HTML fragments.

3. **Focus target priority is logically correct.** Next sibling > previous sibling > add-todo input. routing.content:
**I consent to Task #3 (d86e6b7).**

From a domain architecture perspective, this is a pure progressive enhancement -- no domain, service, or infrastructure changes. My review notes:

1. **No domain layer impact.** The delete handler (`post_delete_todo` in routes/todos.rs) is unchanged. The server still returns an empty body for HTMX delete swaps. The focus management is purely client-side behavior responding to the existing HTMX swap lifecycle.

2. **The detection logic is sound.** The script checks two conditions to identify a delete operation: (a) the target has class `todo-item` (matching `todo_item.html:1`), and (b) the response body is empty (which is what `post_delete_todo` returns for HTMX requests). This correctly distinguishes deletes from toggles or other swaps that return HTML fragments.

3. **Focus target priority is logically correct.** Next sibling > previous sibling > add-todo input. This follows natural reading order and handles the "list is now empty" edge case by falling back to the `#title` input, which is the right call -- the user's next action after deleting the last item would be to add a new one.

4. **The `pendingFocusTarget` pattern is a clean way to bridge the before-swap and after-settle lifecycle.** Capture the target while DOM siblings are still available, apply focus after the swap has settled. The variable is nulled after use to prevent stale references.

5. **`tabindex="-1"` for programmatic focus on non-interactive `<li>` elements** is correct -- the todo items are `<li>` elements which aren't natively focusable.

No domain concerns. Clean progressive enhancement.

content.text: Consent sent for Task #3. Pure client-side focus management -- no domain impact, correct detection of delete swaps, logical focus target priority. Standing by for Task #4 (empty state transition).
